card_selection_guide.description: Which card to use for each spending category to maximize rewards

groceries_us.runner_up_rate: 5% when rotating category is active (Q1 2026), 1% otherwise
groceries_us.note: After $6K annual grocery cap on Amex, switch to Discover if in-season or Citi for 1x miles

wholesale_clubs.runner_up_note: Amex may not be accepted at Costco. Use Discover or Visa cards at Costco.
wholesale_clubs.note: Costco only accepts Visa — use Chase Sapphire (1x) or Atlas (1x) if Discover not in rotating season

dining.rate: 2x (was 3x through Aug 2025, dropped to 2x from Oct 2025 — dynamic top category shifted)
dining.note: Atlas and Sapphire are now tied at 2x for dining. Atlas still preferred: $0 annual fee, and Atlas points redeem at 1cpp for hotels vs Chase UR at ~1.25cpp via portal. Monitor Atlas rate — if dining goes back to 3x, advantage returns. Chase Sapphire's value proposition is now very thin.

streaming_us.note: Amex BCP is best year-round for streaming

gas_stations_us.note: Discover beats Amex when gas is rotating category

transit_rideshare.note: Citi Lyft credit ($10/mo after 3 rides) stacks — use Citi for Lyft specifically

american_airlines.rate: 4x AA miles (10x on AA hotels/rental cars)
american_airlines.note: Always use Citi for AA purchases

flights_non_aa.note: Must book through Amex Travel for 5x. Direct booking = 1x.

hotels.note: Use Biz Plat FHR/THC bookings to also earn $600 hotel credit

rental_cars.rate: 10x on rental cars booked via AA
rental_cars.runner_up_note: Also use Citi Avis/Budget credit ($120/yr) for prepaid rentals
rental_cars.note: Book through AA for 10x miles + use Avis/Budget credit separately

wine_spirits.note: No card has a bonus category for this. All earn base rate. Citi 1x miles slightly more valuable than 1% cashback. Consider if Atlas top category could cover this.
wine_spirits.optimization_note: Large spend category ($3,600+/yr in 2026) — no great option. This is a portfolio gap.

insurance.note: Massive category ($24K+/yr). All cards earn base rate. A 2% flat card would save $240/yr vs 1x miles. Portfolio gap.
insurance.optimization_note: BIGGEST optimization opportunity if a 2% card is added to portfolio

online_shopping.rate: 5% if Amazon is rotating category, otherwise 1%
online_shopping.note: Check Discover calendar quarterly for Amazon/online categories

business_purchases_5k_plus.note: Up to $2M/yr. Great for large business expenses.

dell_technology.note: Always use Biz Plat for Dell purchases to earn credits

wireless_phone.note: Always pay wireless bill with Biz Plat for $120/yr credit

grubhub.note: Always use Citi for Grubhub orders

everything_else.note: AA miles generally worth 1.5-1.7cpp vs 1% cashback. Citi wins for uncategorized spend.

international.note: Never use Amex Blue Cash Preferred internationally

ynab_integration.description: YNAB-driven offer selection and card optimization workflow

pre_walkthrough_protocol.step_1: Pull last 90 days of YNAB transactions across all linked accounts
pre_walkthrough_protocol.step_2: Build vendor-frequency map: payee → count, total_spend, avg_transaction, card_used
pre_walkthrough_protocol.step_3: Build category-spend map: category → total_spend, primary_card_used, optimal_card
pre_walkthrough_protocol.step_4: Identify recurring charges: payees appearing 2+ months in a row
pre_walkthrough_protocol.step_5: Flag suboptimal card usage: any recurring charge where card_used != optimal_card per category rules

offer_selection_logic.auto_add: Vendor appears in YNAB transactions within last 90 days, OR vendor is a known recurring charge
offer_selection_logic.likely_add: Vendor is in a category where David spends $50+/month (e.g., dining, grocery, gas), OR offer value exceeds $25 with reasonable likelihood of use
offer_selection_logic.skip: No YNAB spend history, vendor not in any active spend category, offer value under $10 with no foreseeable use
offer_selection_logic.always_add: Free money offers (e.g., Orkin $30 back on $30 = free, CNN/discovery+ subscription credits), TurboTax (annual tax filing), any vendor where David has an active subscription

card_migration_rules.dining: Should be on Atlas Visa (2x) or Chase Sapphire (2x) — never on Citi (1x) or Amex (1x unless BCP for specific vendors)
card_migration_rules.groceries: Should be on Amex BCP (6% up to $6K) then Discover (5% when rotating) — never on Citi or Chase
card_migration_rules.streaming: Should be on Amex BCP (6%) — never on Plat (1x) or Chase (1x)
card_migration_rules.gas: Should be on Amex BCP (3%) or Discover (5% when rotating) — never on Citi (1x)
card_migration_rules.flights_other: Amex Biz Plat via Amex Travel (5x)
card_migration_rules.rideshare: Lyft → Citi (stacks $10/mo credit + 1x miles). Other rideshare → Amex BCP (3%)
card_migration_rules.subscriptions: Map each sub to its best card. Digital entertainment subs → Amex Plat (up to $25/mo credit). Other subs → category-based optimization.
card_migration_rules.insurance: Portfolio gap — use Citi (1x miles) until a 2% card is added

portfolio_gaps.issue: No 2% flat-rate card for large uncategorized spend
portfolio_gaps.impact: Insurance alone is $24K+/yr earning 1x (~1%). A 2% card would double returns on this category.
portfolio_gaps.annual_cost: $240+/yr in missed rewards on insurance alone
portfolio_gaps.recommendation: Consider Citi Double Cash (2%) or Wells Fargo Active Cash (2%) for insurance and other uncategorized spend

portfolio_gaps.issue: Chase Sapphire base card has limited value
portfolio_gaps.impact: Only 2x on dining (good), 1x everything else (poor). No transfer partners without CSP/CSR.
portfolio_gaps.recommendation: Consider product-changing to Chase Sapphire Preferred ($95/yr) for 3x dining, 2x travel, and transfer partner access — or use dining spend on Atlas if it auto-assigns 3x
